content.full_text:
FPPC
May 14 11:41 AM '90

Senator David Roberti
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE, CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE

May 10, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" St., Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Request for Formal Written Advice

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I am an elected state officer. I am the legislator who carried the legislation which placed Proposition 107 on the June 5 state ballot. Proposition 107 is a bond measure for funds to assist in relieving the problems of the homeless in California.

I control my own committee, House the Homeless, which is supporting the ballot measure. I am also on the honorary fundraising committee for HOMEPAC, a sponsored committee of the California Center on Housing Priorities, which is a general purpose ballot measure committee. HOMEPAC is currently working for passage of Proposition 107.

I wish to avoid any issues of involvement of my State Senate staff with respect to the bond measure campaign. Hence, in order to handle the communications with HOMEPAC without using Senate staff, I would like to have my controlled committee, House the Homeless, contract with a person on a part-time basis, to handle those communications. This would involve such things as scheduling for fund-raising events. The expected amount of time would be a maximum of 15 hours per month. The contract expressly state that this person would not in any way direct, instruct or otherwise control the activities of HOMEPAC's campaign to pass Proposition 107.

My question is whether, under the foregoing facts, I am correct in my understanding that this would not make HOMEPAC a committee controlled by me. I particularly base that understanding on your previous advice letter to Mr. Patrick, on behalf of Senator Boatwright.

Reply to: P.O. Box 2856, Los Angeles, CA 90028

NOT PRINTED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE

Kathryn Donovan
May 10, 1990
Page 2


I realize you and your staff are very busy, however, because of
the closeness of the June 5 election and the need for HOMEPAC to
engage in active fund raising between now and then, I would
greatly appreciate receiving interim telephone advice as soon as
practicable so that I may proceed.

Sincerely,

[signature]

DAVID ROBERTI
President pro Tempore

DR:cmrmd

sections.question: Whether, under the foregoing facts, I am correct in my understanding that this would not make HOMEPAC a committee controlled by me. I particularly base that understanding on your previous advice letter to Mr. Patrick, on behalf of Senator Boatwright.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Roberti seeks confirmation that hiring a part-time contractor through his controlled committee (House the Homeless) to handle communications with HOMEPAC, a sponsored committee supporting Proposition 107, would not result in HOMEPAC being considered a committee controlled by him.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Senator Roberti to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or ADVICE section is present in this document. The letter references a prior FPPC advice letter to Mr. Patrick on behalf of Senator Boatwright as supporting authority.

embedding.summary: Senator David Roberti requests formal written advice from the FPPC regarding whether contracting a part-time person through his controlled committee (House the Homeless) to handle communications with HOMEPAC would make HOMEPAC a controlled committee. He cites time constraints due to the approaching June 5 election and requests interim telephone advice.
